purpose: End-user-friendly macOS double-clickable launcher. Unlike launch_synapse.command (self-relocating, dev mode), this one MUST live in the Synapse repo (it cd's to its own directory) and runs `npm start` (production build via Vite + Electron). Performs the same port-3456 conflict check as start.sh and refuses to launch when the app is already running. Adds a 'Press any key to close...' prompt on error so the Terminal window stays visible for the user.
gotchas: Filename CONTAINS A SPACE — when scripting it from another shell, always quote the path: `"Start Synapse.command"`.
Cannot be relocated — `cd "$DIR"` resolves to the script's own directory, so moving this file out of the Synapse repo breaks it (use launch_synapse.command for the movable version).
Runs `npm start` (production build), NOT `npm run dev` — slower iteration but matches the README's official launch flow.
If `npm start` fails partway through, the .command Terminal window may close before the user sees the error — consider adding a trap-on-error keypress.
patterns: Production-launch pattern: build via Vite, then exec Electron — same as `npm start`.
Banner pattern: prints a divider/title before any action so the Terminal output is recognizable at a glance.
Conflict-check pattern: same port-busy check as start.sh, with the same 'use Stop Synapse.command' guidance.
conventions: Title bars use `=` characters and centered spacing.
Echo blank lines before/after status messages for readability in the Terminal popup window.
